questions.question: In what proportion should a grocer mix wheat at ₹12 per kg and ₹25 per kg so as to make a mixture of worth ₹20 per kg?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 5 : 8. Given: A grocer mixes wheat at ₹12 per kg and ₹25 per kg so as to make a mixture of worth ₹20 per kg Explanation: ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Ashwini%20Thakur_132024_08_13_19_40_43.08) Hence, the required ratio is 5: 8

questions.question: The price of an article is increased by r%. The new price was decreased by r% later. Now the latest price is Rs.1. What was the original price of the article?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(\frac{10000}{10000-r^2}\). The original price of the article = 1 × 100/(100 + r) × 100/(100 - r) = \(\frac{10000}{10000-r^2}\)

questions.question: A, B and C together can complete a work in 10 days. A and B together can do the same work in 12 days. If A, B, and C received ₹9,000 for doing the work together, then what was the share of C from the above amount?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. ₹1,500. Let the total work to be done (LCM of 10 and 12) be 60 units. Efficiency of A, B and C = 60/10 = 6 units/day Efficiency of A and B = 60/12 = 5 units/day Efficiency of C = 6 - 5 = 1 unit/day The wages are distributed in the ratio of their efficiencies. Share of C = (9000/6) × 1 = 1500

questions.question: Simplify the following: 7(cot 11° cot 13º cot 19° cot 29° cot 61° cot 71° cot 77° cot 79°)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 7. Given, 7(cot 11° cot 13º cot 19° cot 29° cot 61° cot 71° cot 77° cot 79°) ⇒ 7(cot 11° cot 13º cot 19° cot 29° tan 29° tan 19° tan 13° tan 11°) = 7

questions.question: The cost of manufacture of a tape recorder is ₹ 3500. The manufacturer fixes the marked price 30% above the cost price and allows a discount in such as to get a profit of 5%. Find the discount percentage.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 19.23%. C.P. = Rs. 3500 The manufacturer marks up the price by 30% above the cost price M.P. = C.P. + 30% of C.P. ⇒ M.P. = 3500 + 0.3 × 3500 ⇒ M.P. = 3500 + 1050 ⇒ M.P. = Rs.4550 The manufacturer wants to make a 5% profit on the cost price Profit = 0.05 × 3500 = Rs.175 ⇒ S.P. = C.P. + Profit ⇒ S.P. = 3500 + 175 = Rs.3675 Discount = M.P. - S.P. ⇒ Discount = 4550 - 3675 = Rs.875 ⇒ Discount% = (Discount/M.P.) × 100 ⇒ Discount% = 875/4550 × 100 = 19.23 Thus, discount% is approximately 19.23%

questions.question: The average of 9 numbers is 15. If the average of the first five numbers is 13 and the average of the last five numbers is 17, then what is the fifth number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 15. We know that, Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations Now, The average of 9 numbers is 15 The sum of 9 numbers = 9 × 15 = 135 The average of the first five numbers is 13 The sum of first five numbers = 5 × 13 = 65 The average of the last five numbers is 17 The sum of last five numbers = 5 × 17 = 85 Hence, the fifth number = (65 + 85) - 135 = 150 - 135 = 15

questions.question: When a positive integer 'n' is divided by 12, the remainder is 5. What will be the remainder if 8n2 +7 is divided by 12?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 3. Let the positive integer be 17. 8n2 + 7 is divided by 12 ⇒ 8(289) + 7 = 2319 is divided by 12 ⇒ (12 × 193 + 3) is divided by 12 So, the remainder is 3. Hence the required answer is 3.

questions.question: What is the average of the first six even numbers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 7. The first six even numbers are 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations Average = (2 + 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 + 12)/6 Average = 42/6 = 7

questions.question: Three circles of radius 4 cm are kept touching each other. The string is tightly tied around these three circles. What is the length of the string?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. (24 + 8π) Cm. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Screenshot%202025-03-05%201955232025_03_05_19_57_06.png) Radius of each circle = 4 cm Length of the string = 2πr + 6r = 2π × 4 + 6 × 4 = (24 + 8π) Cm

questions.question: The volume of a sphere of radius 4.2 cm is: Use π = 22/7
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e 310.464 cm3. The volume of the sphere = \(\frac{4}{3}\)πr3 ⇒ 4/3 × 22/7 × 4.2 × 4.2 × 4.2 = 310.464 cm3

questions.question: The radius of the base and the height of a closed cylinder are 14 cm and 14 cm, respectively. The total surface area of the cylinder is equal to the area of a circle. What will be the diameter of the circle? [Use π = 22/7 ]
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 56 cm. The total surface area of a cylinder = 2πr(r + h) = 2π × (14) × (14 + 14) = 2π(14) × (28) = 784π sq.cm The area of a circle = πr2 According to the question πr2 = 784π r2= 784 r = √(784) = 28 d = 2r = 2(28) = 56 cm Therefore, the diameter of the circle is 56 cm.

questions.question: What is the value of sin 37° cot 40° cot 60° tan 50° sec 53°?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. \(\frac{tan^2 50°}{\sqrt{3}}\). ⇒ sin 37° cot 40° cot 60° tan 50° sec 53° ⇒ sin (90 – 53°) cot (90° – 50°) cot 60° tan 50° sec 53° ⇒ cos 53° tan 50° cot 60° tan 50° sec 53° ⇒ \(\frac{tan^2 50°}{\sqrt{3}}\)

questions.question: If (a + b + c) = 16, and (a2 + b2 + c2) = 90, find the value of (ab + bc + ca).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 83. a + b + c = 16 a2 + b2 + c2 = 90 (a + b + c)2 = a2 + b2 + c2 + 2(ab + bc + ca) ⇒ (16)2 = 90 + 2(ab + bc + ca) ⇒ 256 = 90 + 2(ab + bc + ca) ⇒ 2(ab + bc + ca) = 166 ⇒ ab + bc + ca = 83

questions.question: In a given figure, O is the centre of the circle and ∠POQ = 54°. What is the measure (in degrees) of ∠PRQ? ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Screenshot%202025-03-05%201844302025_03_05_18_45_22.png)

So ∠PSQ + ∠PRQ = 180° ∠PRQ = 180° - 27° = 153°

questions.question: Find the greatest value of ‘k’ in a 6-digit number 6745k2 such that the number is divisible by 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e 9. Divisibility rule of 3: A number is divisible by 3 when the sum of the digits of the number is divisible by 3. According to the given question, Given number = 6745k2 Sum of digits = 6 + 7 + 4 + 5 + k + 2 ⇒ 24 + k The numbers that can take place of k are 0, 3, 6, 9. Hence the required answer is 9.

questions.question: The cost price of an article is 60 percent of the marked price. What will be the profit percentage after allowing a discount of 4 percent on the marked price?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 60 percent. Let the M.P. be 100x Then, the C.P. of the article is 60% of x = 0.6x 4% discount on marked price Discount % = (Marked Price - Selling Price)/Marked Price × 100 ⇒ S.P. = x - 4%x ⇒ S.P. = x - 0.04x ⇒ S.P. = 0.96x Profit = S.P. - C.P. ⇒ Profit = 0.96x - 0.6x = 0.36x ⇒ Profit percentage = Profit/C.P. × 100 ⇒ Profit% = 0.36x/0.6x × 100 ⇒ Profit% = 60%

questions.question: Two trains of length 200 m and 400 m run on parallel lines. When they run in the same direction, it takes 30 seconds for the train with the higher speed to overtake the other train, and when they travel in opposite directions, it takes them 6 seconds to cross each other. What are the speeds (in km/h) of the two trains?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 216 and 144. When two trains run in the same direction, their relative speed is equal to the difference between their individual speeds. When two trains run in opposite directions, their relative speed is equal to the sum of their individual speeds. Let the speed of the faster train be x m/sec and the speed of the slower train be y m/sec. Total distance = 200 + 400 = 600 m Now, 600 = (x - y) × 30 ⇒ x - y = 20 ...(i) Also, 600 = (x + y) × 6 ⇒ x + y = 100 ...(ii) Now, adding equation (i) and equation (ii), ⇒ 2x = 120 ⇒ x = 60 m/sec ⇒ x = 60 × (18/5) ⇒ x = 216 km/h Also, y = 40 m/sec ⇒ y = 40 × (18/5) ⇒ y = 144 km/h

questions.question: Ratio of present ages of Tushar, Garima and Saurav is 5 : 7 : 4. If the sum of their ages after 9 years will be 139 years then what was the age of Garima 5 years ago?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 44 years Suppose the present ages of Tushar, Garima and Saurav are 5x, 7x and 4x respectively. Given: sum of their ages after 9 years will be 139 years So, (5x + 7x + 4x) + 9 × 3 = 139 ⇒ 16x = 139 – 27 ⇒ 16x = 112 ⇒ x = 7 Hence, age of Garima 5 years ago = 7 × 7 – 5 = 44 years

questions.question: Half of the perimeter of a square is 50 cm. What is the length of each side of the square?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 25 cm. Half of the perimeter of the square is 50 cm Perimeter of the square = 4 × side ⇒ (4 × side)/2 = 50 ⇒ 2 × side = 50 ⇒ side = 25 cm

questions.question: Pipes A, B and C can fill a tank in 15, 30 and 40 hours, respectively. Pipes A, B and C are opened at 6 a.m., 8 a.m. and 10 a.m., respectively, on the same day. When will the tank be full?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 3 : 20 p.m. Pipes A, B, and C can fill a tank in 15, 30 and 40 hours, LCM(15, 30, 40) = 120 = Total work Efficiency of A = 120/15 = 8 units Efficiency of B = 120/30 = 4 units Efficiency of C = 120/40 = 3 units So, from 6 am - 8 am, only A works Work completed = 2 × 8 = 16 units From 8 am - 10 am, A and B work Work completed = 2 × 12 = 24 units Work remaining = 120 - 16 - 24 = 80 units completed by A, B, and C together. Time required = 80/(8 + 4 + 3) = 5(1/3) hours = 5 hours 20 minutes So, Tank fills = 10 am + 5 hours 20 minutes = 3 pm 20 minutes

questions.question: In ∆ABC, AB = 7cm, BC = 10cm, and AC = 8cm. If AD is the angle bisector of ∠BAC, where D is a point on BC, then DC (in cm) =?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. \(\frac{16}{3}\). ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Screenshot%202025-03-05%201834142025_03_05_18_35_00.png) BD = 10 - DC AB/AC = BD/DC ⇒ 7/8 = (10 - DC)/DC ⇒ DC = 80/15 = 16/3

questions.question: The ratio of men and women in a village is 7 : 6. If 10 men join the village and 15 women leave the village, the ratio becomes 4 : 3. Find the previous population of the village.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 390. Let the number of men and women be 7x and 6x respectively. Ratio when 10 men join and 15 women leave = (7x + 10)/(6x – 15) According of condition, (7x + 10)/(6x – 15) = 4/3 3(7x + 10) = 4(6x – 15) 21x + 30 = 24x – 60 3x = 90 x = 30 Total population = 6x + 7x = 13x = 13 × 30 = 390

questions.question: By selling an article for ₹2352, one gains ₹752. What is the gain percent?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 47%. C.P. = S.P. - Gain ⇒ C.P. = 2352 - 752 ⇒ C.P. = Rs.1600 Profit% = (Profit/C.P.) × 100 ⇒ Profit% = 752/1600 × 100 ⇒ Profit% = 752/16 ⇒ Profit = 47%

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 64. x = 3y + 4 ⇒ x - 3y = 4 ..(1) a3 - b3 = a3 - b3 - 3ab(a - b) Applying cube on both sides in eq(1) - ⇒ (x - 3y)3 = 43 ⇒ x3 - (3y)3 - 3 × x × 3y(x - 3y) = 64 ⇒ x3 - 27y3 - 3 × x × 3y(4) = 64 ⇒ x3 - 27y3 - 36xy = 64

questions.question: P, Q, and R can complete a piece of work in 10 days, 15 days, and 20 days, respectively. If they work together, in how many days can they finish the same work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 4\(\frac{8}{13}\). P, Q, and R can complete a piece of work in 10 days, 15 days, and 20 days. Total work L.C.M (10, 15, and 20) = 60 unit The efficiency of P = 60/10 = 6 units/day The efficiency of Q = 60/15 = 4 units/day The efficiency of R = 60/20 = 3 units/day Time taken by all three to complete the work = 60/(6 + 4 + 3) ⇒ 60/13 = 4\(\frac{8}{13}\)

questions.question: If the total income of the family was Rs 1,00,000, how much money (in Rs) was spent on the education of the children?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 12,000. According to the given pie-chart, Total income of the family = Rs 1,00,000 Percentage spent on children's education = 12% The amount was spent on the education of the children = 100000 × 12/100 = 12000

questions.question: Three years ago, Rahul borrowed a sum of money at a compound interest rate of 10% per annum compounding annually. If the present value of that borrowed money is Rs.11,979, how much money was borrowed by Rahul?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Rs.9,000. A = P(1 + R/100)T where, A = Amount, P = Principal, R = rate, and T = Time ⇒ 11979 = P(1 + 10/100)3 ⇒ 11979 = P(1 + 1/10)3 ⇒ 11979 = P(11/10)3 ⇒ (11979 × 10 × 10 × 10)/(11 × 11 × 11) = P ⇒ P = Rs.9000

questions.question: A policeman notices a thief from a distance of 200 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and the policeman run at 10 km/h and 11 km/h, respectively. What is the distance (in meters) between them after 9 minutes?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 50. Thief's speed = 10 km/hr = 10 × 1000/60 = 1000/6 = 500/3 m/min Policeman's speed = 11 km/hr = 11 × 1000/60 = 1100/6 = 550/3 m/min Thus, Distance traveled by Thief in 9 minutes = 500/3 × 9 = 1500 meters Distance traveled by policeman in 9 minutes = 1100/6 × 9 = 1650 meters Initial distance = 200 meters Distance covered by policeman in 9 minutes = 1650 - 1500 = 150 meters Thus, Total distance between them after 9 minutes = 200 - 150 = 50 meters

questions.question: The difference in compound interest, under annual compounding, and simple interest on a certain sum at the same rate of interest in 2 years is 144% of the sum. Find the rate of interest per annum.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 120%. Difference (CI - SI) for 2 years = P(r/100)2 144% of P = P(r/100)2 144/100 = (r2/10000) r2 = 14400 r = 120%

questions.question: A started a business with a capital of Rs.4,50,000. After 3 months, B joined him with a certain amount of capital. At the end of a year of A’s starting the business, the profit was shared in the ratio 3 : 2. How much (in Rs.) did B invest?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 4,00,000. Let x be the amount B invested. A starts with a capital of Rs. 4,50,000 and remains invested for 12 months. B joins after 3 months and remains invested for 12 − 3 = 9 months. ⇒ A/B = (450000 × 12)/9x ⇒ 3/2 = (450000 × 12)/9x ⇒ 9x = (450000 × 12) × 2/3 ⇒ x = 10800000/27 ⇒ x = 400000 Hence, B invested Rs. 4,00,000

questions.question: What is the median of the following distribution? Class 100-150 150-200 200-250 250-300 -00-350 Frequency 22 18 25 15 20
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 220. Class 100-150 150-200 200-250 250-300 -00-350 Frequency 22 18 25 15 20 Cumulative Frequency (CF) 22 40 65 80 100 Total frequency(N) = 100 ⇒ N/2 = 100/2 = 50 The median class is the class where the cumulative frequency exceeds 50. From the table, Median Class = 200-250 Median = L + \(\frac{(\frac{N}{2} - CF)}{f }\)× h Here, L = Lower boundary of the median class, N = Total frequency, CF = Cumulative frequency of the class preceding the median class, f = Frequency of the median class, and h = Class width Median = 200 + (100/2 - 40)/25 × 50 Median = 200 + (50 - 40) × 2 Median = 200 + 10 × 2 Median = 220

questions.question: If an eleven-digit number 6578x43267y is divisible by 72, then the value will be:\((\sqrt{x + 6y})\)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 4. 6578x43267y is divisible by 72 For divisible by 72 the no. should be divided by 8 & 9. The divisibility rule of 8 is if the last three digits of a no. is divisible by 8 then the whole no. is divisible by 8. The divisibility rule of 9 is if the sum of digits of a no. is divisible by 9 then the whole no. is divisible by 9. Now, the last 3 digits 67y is divisible by 8 when y = 2 The sum of digits = 6 + 5 + 7 + 8 + x + 4 + 3 + 2 + 6 + 7 + 2 = 50 + x This is divisible by 9 if x = 4 The value will be:\((\sqrt{x + 6y})\) = \((\sqrt{4 + 6 × 2})\) = 4

questions.question: Directions: In the question below given a statement followed by two Conclusion numbered I and II. Decide which of the suggested option follow(s). if neither I nor II follows

questions.question: Read the following information carefully and answer the question given below. Five friends Arun, Bala, Cheenu, Dinesh and Elango are sitting on a bench facing north. Arun is sitting next to Bala. Dinesh is sitting next to Cheenu. Dinesh is not sitting with Elango. Elango is on the left end of the bench. Cheenu is on the second position from the right. Arun sits to the right of Bala. What is the position of Dinesh?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Extreme right ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Shraddha_RRB%20JE%20PY%201_052024_10_07_13_27_10.10) Dinesh is on the extreme right.

questions.question: Students are sitting in a row in which the position of X is 17th from the left and that of Y is 14th from the right. If they interchange their positions, X’s position becomes 7th from the left. How many students are there in the row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 20 ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_18-10-2024_D52024_10_18_15_07_21.png) That means Y’s original position was 7th from left and 14th from right. Hence, total number of students in row = (7 + 14) – 1 = 20.

questions.question: Select the option that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. TY42, RA51, OC79, KE144, ?

P, Q, R, S and T are sitting in a straight line, facing north. S is an immediate neighbour of both P and R. R is an immediate neighbour of both S and Q. Q is an immediate neighbour of both R and T. Who are the immediate neighbours of Q?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. R and T. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/RRB%20Group%20D%20-%20Gagan%20Py%201_24-10-2024_D42024_10_25_13_27_32.png) R and T are the immediate neighbours of Q.

questions.question: Directions: Two statements are given followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All games are tournaments. Some tournaments are matches. Conclusions: I. All games are matches. II. No game is a tournament.
options: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows.
Both Conclusions I and II follow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Neither Conclusion I nor II follows. The least possible Venn diagram is shown below: ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Shraddha_CPO%202024%20PY3%20-%20LR_132024_08_14_10_09_55.08) Conclusions: I. All games are matches - Does not follow. (It is possible but not definite, as no direct relationship between the two is given). II. No game is a tournament - Does not follow. (As all games are tournaments, so no game is a tournament is not true.)

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged in the following equation to make it correct? 24 ÷ 2 + 13 - 54 × 2 = 34
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. × and ÷. Given equation: 24 ÷ 2 + 13 - 54 × 2 = 34 From option 2 by interchanging signs × and ÷, we get 24 × 2 + 13 - (54 ÷ 2) = (24 × 2) + 13 - 27 = (48 + 13) - 27 = 61 - 27 = 34

questions.question: Kumar's father Arun's son Shankar's wife Hema is the daughter in law of Kumar's father's wife Prema. How is Prema related to Kumar?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Mother. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_22-04-2025_D312025_04_22_17_19_53.png) From the above tree, Prema is the mother of Kumar.

questions.question: Directions: In each of the following questions, a statement is given followed by two assumptions. You have to decide which assumption(s) are implicit in the given statement. Statement: Complaints by professors led to the ban on the use of mobile phones inside colleges, which tend to create disturbance in a class. Assumptions: I. The professors were distracted from the use of mobile phones by children in a class. II. Disruption in a class led to poor outcomes and underperformance by children.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only I is implicit I. Implicit (The statement mentions that professors complained and that mobile phones cause disturbance. It is fair to assume that the source of the complaint was the distraction caused by mobile phone use by students, which affected the professors’ ability to teach effectively.) II. Not implicit (This assumption goes beyond what is implied. The reason for the ban, according to the statement, is classroom disturbance, but it doesn't say why that matters (such as leading to poor outcomes).

questions.question: In a certain code, 'TEMPER' is written as 'IVKNVG', and 'TABLE' is written as 'VOYZG'. Then, how is 'TUBELIGHT' written in the same code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. GSTROVYFG. The pattern is as follows: The opposite letters of the letters in a word are taken and written from right to left. TEMPER → T ⇔ G, E ⇔ V, M ⇔ N, P ⇔ K, E ⇔ V, R ⇔ I ⇒ GVNKVI ⇒ IVKNVG TABLE → T ⇔ G, A ⇔ Z, B ⇔ Y, L ⇔ O, E ⇔ V ⇒ GZYOV ⇒ VOYZG Similarly, TUBELIGHT → T ⇔ G, U ⇔ F, B ⇔ Y, E ⇔ V, L ⇔ O, I ⇔ R, G ⇔ T, H ⇔ S, T ⇔ G ⇒ GFYVORTSG ⇒ GSTROVYFG Hence, the correct answer is GSTROVYFG.

questions.question: Select the triad in which the numbers are related to each other in the same way as are the numbers of the given triads. (251, 268, 134), (76, 86, 43) (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13, such as adding /deleting /multiplying, etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
If it will be Tuesday day after tomorrow, then what was the day of the week 68 days ago from today?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Tuesday. Given: The day after tomorrow = Tuesday Tomorrow = Tuesday - 1 = Monday Today = Tuesday - 2 = Sunday Now, 68 days ago from today, So, 68 days = 68 ÷ 7 = 9 week and 5 odd day Then 68 days ago from today = Sunday - 5 = Tuesday.

questions.question: A tourist wanted to see the planetarium in a city. He walked from the railway station in the east direction and arrived at a pond. He turned left and then at the second corner of the pond, he turned right. Soon he reached another corner of the pond. He turned left and arrived at the planetarium. In what direction was the railway station situated with respect to the planetarium?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. South-west. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/HRQ-7307992025_04_30_17_50_17.png) Thus, the railway station is situated in a south-west direction with respect to the planetarium.

questions.question: Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G live on seven different floors of the same building. The lowermost floor is numbered 1, the floor above it is numbered 2, and so on till the topmost floor is numbered 7. F lives on the topmost floor. B lives on floor number 3. Four persons live below A. Two persons live between A and E. D lives above C. C does not live on odd number floor. Who lives on the lowermost floor?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. G. Floor Person 7 F 6 D 5 A 4 C 3 B 2 E 1 G Thus, G lives on the lowermost floor.

questions.question: ‘Fragile’ is related to ‘Breakable’ in the same way as ‘Frown’ is related to ‘________’.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Anger. Fragile and Breakable are synonyms. Fragile means something that can easily be broken. Similarly, Frown and Anger are synonyms. Frown means to show you are angry.

questions.question: All of the letters in the word ‘GLORIOUS’ are arranged in alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is third from the left end and the one which is fifth from the right end in the new letter-cluster thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Two. Given G L O R I O U S Alphabetical order G I L O O R S U Here, 'L' is third from the left end and the letter 'O' is fifth from the right end. Thus, two letters come between L and O (M, N).

questions.question: How many such pairs of letters are there in the word PLATE (in both forward and backward directions) which have as many letters between them in the word as there are in the English alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. None. Word: PLATE There are none pairs of letters are there in the word which have as many letters between them in the word as there are in the English alphabetical order.

questions.question: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below: In a certain code language, 'There is Park' is written as ‘nup yex ace’ 'People in Schools' is written as ‘ybx cvu vcl’ 'Several Schools Are Present There' is written as ‘cal del vuc ybx yex’ 'Park is Dark' is written as ‘nup cig ace’ 'Dark People in Several Park' is written as ‘vcl cal nup cig cvu’ What could the code ‘cig ybx gkl vcl’ stand for in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Near People Dark Schools. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_18_04-2025_D312025_04_18_19_38_40.png) ‘cig ybx gkl vcl’ is written as Near People Dark Schools.

So, Statement I alone is not sufficient. Statement II: We do not have information of Mahesh. So, Statement II alone is not sufficient. Combining Statement I and II: ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Nita_SSC%20Selection%20Post%208%20Feb_11%20July%202025_D852025_07_11_17_20_41.png) Hence, the correct answer is Statement I and II both are necessary to answer the question.

questions.question: Suresh switched on a Surya tube light at 8:15:40 pm. And switched it off at 8:42:45 am on the next day. How long time did the tube light last? (In hours)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 12 hours 27 minutes 05 seconds. The tubelight last from 8:15:40 pm to 8:42:45 am = 12:27:05 hours = 12 h 27 min 05 sec Hence, the correct answer is 12 hours 27 minutes 05 seconds.

questions.question: At what time between 9 and 10 o'clock will the hands of a watch be together?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e.\(49^{\frac{1}{11}}\text{ min. past 9}\). Both hands have 15 minutes of space at 9 o'clock. To be together between 9 and 10 minutes hand has to gain 45 min. Now, the minute hand gains 55 min in 60 min. It will gain 45 min (60/55)×45 = \(49^{\frac{1}{11}}\text{ min. past 9}\). Therefore, the hands are together at \(49^{\frac{1}{11}}\text{ min. past 9}\).

questions.question: Which Later Mughal ruler’s reign saw the rise of the Nawab of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Farrukhsiyar. Farrukhsiyar's reign (1713–1719) marked the critical rise of the Nawab of Bengal as an autonomous power. It was during his rule that Murshid Quli Khan was appointed as Bengal's governor in 1717. Farrukhsiyar granted him the dual role of Diwan (revenue chief) and Subahdar (governor), enabling him to consolidate authority. Murshid Quli Khan later declared de facto independence, founding the Nawab dynasty of Bengal. Farrukhsiyar’s weak governance and reliance on manipulative nobles accelerated Mughal decline, allowing regional powers like Bengal to break free. His reign thus became a turning point in India’s shift from centralized Mughal rule to fragmented princely states. Explanation of Other Rulers: • Shah Alam II (1759–1806): His reign witnessed the continued decline of Mughal authority and increasing British intervention, especially after the Battle of Buxar (1764). By then, the Nawab of Bengal was already well-established. • Muhammad Shah (1719–1748): Though his reign saw the weakening of central power and the rise of regional powers, the Nawab of Bengal had already risen under Farrukhsiyar. • Bahadur Shah I (1707–1712): His rule was marked by efforts to consolidate the empire after Aurangzeb’s death, but the Nawab of Bengal had not yet gained significant autonomy during his time.

questions.question: What are included in the Triratnas (Three Jewels) of Jainism?
options: Right faith, right knowledge, right conduct
Right view, right speech, right action
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Right faith, right knowledge, right conduct​​​​​​. The Triratnas (Three Jewels) of Jainism are the foundational principles for achieving liberation (moksha): • Right Faith (Samyak Darshan) – Unshakable belief in the teachings of the Tirthankaras. • Right Knowledge (Samyak Jnana) – Correct understanding of Jain doctrines, including karma, soul, and reality. • Right Conduct (Samyak Charitra) – Ethical living through ahimsa (non-violence), truthfulness, and asceticism.

questions.question: Who among the following became the Indian Governor General after India's independence?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Chakravarti Rajagopalchari. After India's independence in 1947, the first Indian Governor-General was Chakravarti Rajagopalachari. • Lord Mountbatten was the first Governor-General of independent India (1947–1948), but he was British. • C. Rajagopalachari became the first and only Indian Governor-General in 1948, serving until 1950. • In 1950, when India became a republic, the post of Governor-General was replaced by the President of India, and Dr. Rajendra Prasad became the first President.

questions.question: Who among the following was the President of the Lucknow session of the Indian National Congress in 1916?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Ambika Charan Majumdar. Ambika Charan Majumdar, who presided over the historic 1916 Lucknow Session of the Indian National Congress. Key Significance of the Session: • Lucknow Pact: This session marked the landmark reconciliation between the Congress and the All-India Muslim League (led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah), agreeing on joint constitutional reforms and separate electorates for Muslims. • Reunification: It ended the Extremist-Moderate split (1907–1916), with Bal Gangadhar Tilak returning to Congress after years of exile. • Demands: The Congress formally demanded self-government ('Swarajya') for the first time as a unified body. Other Presidents: • Bishan Narayan Dar: 1911 Calcutta Session (where 'Jana Gana Mana' was first sung). • Jawaharlal Nehru: Became president much later (1929, 1936, 1937).

questions.question: When was the Lahore Session of the Indian National Congress held, where everyone present in the meeting pledged to mark 26 January as ‘Independence Day’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 31 December 1929. The Lahore Session of the Indian National Congress, where the historic Purna Swaraj (Complete Independence) resolution was passed and 26 January 1930 was declared as ‘Independence Day’, was held from 29–31 December 1929 in Lahore (now in Pakistan). Key Highlights of the Session: 1. Purna Swaraj Resolution (19 Dec 1929): • The Congress officially demanded complete independence from British rule, abandoning earlier demands for Dominion Status. • The resolution was drafted by Mahatma Gandhi and Jawaharlal Nehru and read out publicly on 26 January 1930 across India. 2. Declaration of 26 January as Independence Day: • The Congress called for nationwide celebrations, with people hoisting the tricolour flag and taking the Independence Pledge. • This date was later commemorated as Republic Day (1950) when India’s Constitution came into effect. 3. Civil Disobedience Movement: • The session authorized Gandhi to launch mass protests, leading to the Dandi March (1930).

questions.question: Who among the following is the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Sukumar Sen. The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen. He was appointed to this position in 1950, shortly after India gained independence. Sukumar Sen played a pivotal role in organizing the first general elections in 1951-52, establishing the framework for the electoral process in the newly independent nation. His contributions were crucial in setting up the Election Commission of India and ensuring the conduct of free and fair elections.

questions.question: Who conducts the election to the "State Legislative Assemblies" in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Election Commission of India. Elections to the State Legislative Assemblies in India are conducted by the Election Commission of India (ECI). The ECI is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering and overseeing the electoral processes in India. It ensures free, fair, and impartial elections to the State Legislative Assemblies, as well as to the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha. The Commission manages the entire electoral process, including voter registration, candidate nominations, and the conduct of the election.

questions.question: The original Constitution of 1950 envisaged a Supreme Court with a Chief Justice and _____________ puisne Judges - leaving it to Parliament to increase this number.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 7. The original Constitution of India, adopted in 1950, envisaged a Supreme Court comprising a Chief Justice and seven puisne judges, making a total of eight judges. This structure established the foundation of the Indian judiciary, allowing Parliament the authority to increase the number of judges as needed. Over the years, amendments have expanded the size of the Supreme Court to accommodate the growing judicial workload and ensure timely justice. This flexibility reflects the dynamic nature of the legal system, adapting to the needs of an evolving democracy and maintaining the rule of law in the country.

questions.question: Which Amendment of the Constitution of India envisages the Gram Sabha as the foundation of the Panchayat Raj System to perform functions and powers entrusted to it by the State Legislatures?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 73rd. The 73rd Amendment of the Constitution of India, enacted in 1992, envisages the Gram Sabha as the foundation of the Panchayat Raj system. This amendment aimed to enhance grassroots democracy and empower local self-governments in rural areas. It establishes a three-tier system of Panchayati Raj institutions, which includes the Gram Panchayat at the village level, the Panchayat Samiti at the block level, and the Zila Parishad at the district level. The Gram Sabha, comprising all eligible voters in a village, is entrusted with specific functions and powers by the State Legislatures, promoting local governance and community participation.

questions.question: Who has the executive authority to advise the State Government on legal matters and to perform other duties of legal character?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Advocate General. The Advocate General of a state holds the executive authority to advise the State Government on legal matters and perform other duties of a legal character. Appointed by the Governor of the state under Article 165 of the Indian Constitution, the Advocate General represents the state in legal cases and offers legal advice to the government. Their role is similar to that of the Attorney General at the national level, and they assist the government in ensuring that its actions are legally sound and constitutionally valid.

questions.question: What is the meaning of geological time?
options: Geological events over millions of years
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Geological events over millions of years. Geological time refers to the vast scale of time used by geologists and paleontologists to describe Earth’s history, from its formation about 4.6 billion years ago to the present. It is divided into eons, eras, periods, epochs, and ages to study the evolution of Earth’s crust, life forms, and major events like continental drift, ice ages, and mass extinctions.

questions.question: Which state of India has the largest irrigated area?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Uttar Pradesh Uttar Pradesh has the largest irrigated area in India due to its extensive network of canals, rivers like the Ganga and Yamuna, and widespread tube well irrigation. Agriculture is the primary occupation, and the state’s fertile alluvial plains support intensive farming of crops like wheat, rice, and sugarcane, making irrigation essential. Other states in brief: • West Bengal – Relies on river-based irrigation for rice cultivation, but total irrigated area is smaller than UP. • Rajasthan – Predominantly arid, irrigation is limited to canal systems like Indira Gandhi Canal. • Punjab – Highly irrigated through canal and tube wells, but smaller in total area than UP. Uttar Pradesh leads in both area and population served by irrigation.

questions.question: Bharatpur National Park is the former name of which of the following national parks?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Keoladeo National Park Bharatpur National Park is the former name of Keoladeo Ghana National Park, located in Rajasthan. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of India’s most important bird sanctuaries, famous for its migratory and resident waterfowl. The park was renamed to highlight the Keoladeo Temple situated nearby. It hosts over 370 species of birds, including the Siberian crane, and provides a critical habitat for wildlife conservation, wetland protection, and eco-tourism, making it a significant ecological landmark in India.

questions.question: Which of the following rocks can float on water due to its porous nature?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Pumice. Pumice is a lightweight, porous volcanic rock that can float on water. It forms when gas-rich lava (like rhyolite) erupts violently, trapping air bubbles as it cools rapidly. This creates a frothy texture with high porosity. Pumice is used in exfoliants, abrasives, and lightweight concrete. Other Options: • Basalt → Dense, non-porous igneous rock. • Obsidian → Glassy volcanic rock with no pores. • Slate → Compact metamorphic rock (from shale).

questions.question: Which of the following crops is NOT suitable to be grown in laterite soils?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Jute. • Laterite soil is a type of soil that forms in tropical and subtropical regions with high rainfall and temperatures. • It's characterized by its reddish colour due to high iron oxide content and is known for being rich in aluminium and iron oxides • It is poor in nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. • Laterite soil is often used for construction and brick-making due to its ability to harden when dry. • In India, it is found in Eastern and Western Ghats, parts of Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, Orissa, Jharkhand, Kerala, and Assam. • Laterite soil is generally suitable for growing crops like tea, coffee, cashew, coconut, rubber, tapioca, pineapple, groundnut etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of genetic industry?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Poultry farming. Genetic industries are those industries which involve breeding of plants and animals with the aim of reproducing and multiplying them for further use. Examples include dairy farming, animal husbandry, fisheries, and poultry farming. These industries depend on the skills of breeding and caring for living organisms. On the other hand, mining, cement manufacturing, and construction of dams are not genetic industries, as they do not involve reproduction of living beings but extraction or processing.

questions.question: How does a budget deficit affect a nation's economy?
options: It indicates a surplus in government spending.
It may lead to increased government borrowing
It results in a decrease in public spending.
It always leads to immediate economic growth.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. It may lead to increased government borrowing. A budget deficit occurs when government expenditure exceeds revenue. This situation may lead to increased government borrowing from the public or financial institutions to meet the gap. Such borrowing can raise interest rates, crowd out private investment, and increase national debt over time. While in some cases, deficit spending can stimulate short-term economic activity, it does not always guarantee immediate growth.

questions.question: How can currency depreciation stimulate an increase in net exports? 1. By reducing export costs 2. By reducing import prices Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1 only. Currency depreciation means the domestic currency loses value against foreign currencies. ● This makes domestic goods cheaper for foreign buyers, thereby reducing export costs and increasing demand for exports. ● However, import prices rise because foreign goods become costlier, which discourages imports. ● Thus, only statement 1 is correct: depreciation boosts net exports by encouraging exports and discouraging imports.

questions.question: Which among the following is an example for 'Indirect Tax'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Customs Duty. An indirect tax is a tax levied on goods and services rather than directly on income or profits. The burden of this tax is generally shifted from the person or entity that initially pays it to the final consumer. In the case of Customs Duty, it is levied on goods imported into or exported from a country. While the importer or exporter pays the duty to the government, they typically pass on this cost to the consumer by including it in the final price of the goods. Therefore, the ultimate financial burden falls on the consumer, making it an indirect tax.

questions.question: When a state is under President's rule, who passes its budget?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Parliament. When a state is under President’s rule, its budget is passed by the Parliament of India. During President’s rule, the state government is temporarily suspended, and the central government takes direct control of the state administration. Since the elected state legislature is either dissolved or suspended, the Parliament assumes the responsibility to approve the state’s budget and other financial matters until normal governance is restored. This ensures continuity in the state’s functioning under central supervision.

questions.question: The total numbers of covalent bond in propane are _________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 10. Propane (C₃H₈) is a hydrocarbon with three carbon atoms and eight hydrogen atoms. Each carbon atom forms four covalent bonds, following the octet rule. The bonding arrangement includes: • Two carbon-carbon (C-C) single covalent bonds. • Eight carbon-hydrogen (C-H) single covalent bonds. Adding these, the total number of covalent bonds in propane is 10. This structure allows propane to be a stable molecule, commonly found as a fuel due to its high energy content upon combustion.

questions.question: Which of the following members of the homologous series of alkanes has the highest melting point?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. C4H10 Among the members of the homologous series of alkanes, the melting point increases as the molecular size increases. Butane (C4H10) has the highest melting point because it has more carbon atoms and a larger molecular structure compared to the others listed, which results in stronger van der Waals forces. These forces require more energy to overcome, leading to a higher melting point. In contrast, smaller molecules like C2H6 (ethane) and C3H8 (propane) have lower melting points.

questions.question: Work done by an object on application of a force would be zero if the displacement of the object is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. zero. Work done by an object on the application of a force would be zero if the displacement of the object is zero. In other words, for work to be done on an object by a force, there must be a component of the force in the direction of the displacement of the object. If the displacement is zero, no work is done, regardless of the magnitude of the force applied.

questions.question: The symbol for resistance (R) is denoted with:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. ![]() . The symbol for resistance (R) is denoted by the ![]() . Resistance represents the opposition to the flow of electric current in a circuit. It is measured in ohms (Ω), where 1 ohm is the resistance that allows a current of 1 ampere to flow through a conductor when a potential difference of 1 volt is applied across its terminals. Resistance affects the performance and efficiency of electrical components and circuits.

questions.question: To which mineral group do galena, chalcopyrite and molybdenite belong?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Sulfide. The minerals galena (PbS), chalcopyrite (CuFeS₂), and molybdenite (MoS₂) all belong to the sulfide group. Sulfides are minerals composed of sulfur (S) combined with metals (e.g., lead in galena, copper and iron in chalcopyrite, molybdenum in molybdenite). Other Options: • Halides contain halogen elements (e.g., fluorite, NaCl). • Phosphides consist of phosphorus bonded with metals (rare in nature). • Oxides are metal-oxygen compounds (e.g., hematite, magnetite). Since all three minerals contain sulfur as the primary anion, the correct answer is Sulfide.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most abundant mineral in our body?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Calcium. The most abundant mineral in the human body is calcium. Calcium is essential for strong bones and teeth, nerve function, muscle contraction, and blood clotting. It makes up about 1.5% of body weight, primarily stored in bones and teeth. Other Options: • Phosphorus is the second most abundant, mainly found in bones and ATP (energy molecules). • Iron is crucial for hemoglobin but is present in much smller amounts. • Copper is classified as a trace mineral, which means the body requires it in only small amounts. Despite this, it plays a vital role in several essential functions, including enzyme activity, iron absorption, and supporting the immune system.

questions.question: Where is the pituitary gland located in the human body?
options: At the base of the brain
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. At the base of the brain. The pituitary gland is a small, pea-sized endocrine gland located at the base of the brain, just below the hypothalamus, within a bony structure called the sella turcica. Often referred to as the "master gland," it regulates many vital body functions by releasing hormones that control other endocrine glands such as the thyroid, adrenal glands, and reproductive organs. It plays a crucial role in growth, metabolism, and reproduction. Other Options: • On the top of kidneys – The adrenal glands are situated here, not the pituitary. • In the intestine – The intestine contains digestive glands but not the pituitary. • Near the spleen – The spleen is part of the immune system and has no connection to the pituitary gland.

questions.question: The pH range of a human body is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 7.35 - 7.45. The correct pH range of the human body is 7.35 - 7.45, which is slightly alkaline. This range is crucial for maintaining homeostasis, as even slight deviations can disrupt enzyme function, cellular processes, and oxygen delivery. Other Options: • 2.35 - 4.45: This highly acidic range is seen in stomach acid (pH ~1.5-3.5), not the body's overall pH. • 5.35 - 6.45: This mildly acidic range is found in urine (pH ~4.5-8.0), but not in blood or tissues. • 8.35 - 9.45: This alkaline range is abnormal for humans; such high pH levels (alkalosis) can cause muscle spasms and confusion. The body tightly regulates pH around 7.35-7.45 through buffers, respiration, and kidney function to ensure optimal physiological functioning.

questions.question: Which of the following language was designed as “programming for the rest of us”?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Hypertalk. • Hypertalk was designed as “programming for the rest of us” by Bill Atkinson for Apple’s Macintosh. • Hypertalk was particularly popular among educators in the 1980s and early ’90s for classroom multimedia presentations.

questions.question: NASSCOM stands for __________.
options: Network Association Of Software & Service Companies
National Act Of Software & Service Companies
Network Access Of Software & Service Companies
National Association Of Software & Service Companies
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. National Association Of Software & Service Companies. Understanding • NASSCOM stands for National Association Of Software & Service Companies. It is a non-profit organisation • It is a trade association of Indian Information Technology and Business Process Outsourcing companies. • NASSCOM was established in 1988.

questions.question: What are the advantages of cyber security?
options: It protects the business against ransomware, malware, social engineering, and phishing.
It gives good protection for both data as well as networks.
Increase recovery time after a breach.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is option 5 i.e. all of these. Benefits of cyber security are as follows: • It protects the business against ransomware, malware, social engineering, and phishing. • It protects end-users. • It gives good protection for both data as well as networks. • Increase recovery time after a breach. • Cybersecurity prevents unauthorized users.

questions.question: A ___________________ is a term used to describe the ability to transfer one file or a segment of data from one format to another format.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. conversion. • A conversion is a term used to describe the ability to transfer one file or a segment of data from one format to another format. • An example of a conversion is converting a text file or CSV file into an Excel spreadsheet file. • In computer science, there are two types of conversion: • Implicit and • Explicit • The term for implicit type conversion is coercion. • Explicit type conversion in some specific way is known as casting.

questions.question: Which of the following festivals is NOT celebrated primarily in Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Losoong. • The Losoong (or Namsoong) festival is a Sikkimese festival primarily celebrated by the Bhutia and Lepcha communities in Sikkim. • It is celebrated to mark the end of the harvest season and the Tibetan New Year. Other Options: Tamla-Du Festival: It is celebrated by the Mishmi tribe in Arunachal Pradesh. The festival is dedicated to appeasing the Gods of earth and water for protection against natural calamities and to foster unity and prosperity within the community. Sangken (or Poi Sangken): It is a Buddhist festival celebrated by the Tai Khamti and Singpho tribes in Arunachal Pradesh and parts of Assam. It is a socio-religious festival that marks the beginning of the New Year. The Ojiyale festival: Also known as Ozele, is a harvest festival celebrated by the Wancho tribe in Arunachal Pradesh, specifically in the Tirap district.

questions.question: Panthi dance is one of the most important types of folk dance of _____________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Chhattisgarh. Panthi dance is one of the most important types of folk dance of Chhattisgarh, India. It is performed by the Satnami community, primarily to honor their spiritual leader, Guru Ghasidas. This dance form is deeply rooted in spiritual and social themes, reflecting the teachings and philosophy of equality and human dignity. Panthi dance is usually performed during religious occasions and festivals, especially on the birth anniversary of Guru Ghasidas. The dancers, dressed in traditional attire, perform to devotional songs with synchronized movements and vibrant expressions, creating a powerful and graceful display of cultural and spiritual unity. Other Options: • Assam: Known for Bihu dance, performed during the spring festival. • Nagaland: Famous for Chang Lo and Aoling dances, performed by tribal communities. • Himachal Pradesh: Known for Nati, a popular group folk dance.

questions.question: Which dance has been included in the list of cultural heritage of mankind since the year 2010?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Kalbelia. Since 2010, Kalbelia dance of Rajasthan has been included in UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. This traditional folk dance is performed by the Kalbelia community, known for their nomadic lifestyle and expertise in snake charming. The dance mimics the graceful movements of a serpent and is accompanied by traditional instruments like the pungi, dholak, and khanjari. Women wear black swirling skirts and perform intricate steps and spins, symbolizing their deep connection to nature and animals. Kalbelia is a vibrant expression of Rajasthan’s cultural identity and a living tradition passed down through generations.

questions.question: Shakoor Khan is associated with which of the following musical instruments?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Sarangi. ​Ustad Shakoor Khan (1905–1975) was a distinguished Indian classical musician renowned for his mastery of the sarangi, a bowed string instrument integral to Hindustani music. He was a prominent figure of the Kirana Gharana and received the Padma Shri, India's fourth-highest civilian award, for his contributions to music. Born into a family of musicians, he was the son of Abdul Zafar Khan and the nephew of Ustad Abdul Wahid Khan. Shakoor Khan accompanied several legendary vocalists, including Bade Ghulam Ali Khan and Amir Khan, and was the first sarangi artist to receive the Padma Shri award.

questions.question: In which city is the headquarters of the European Union located ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Brussels. • The headquarters of the European Union (EU) is located in Brussels, the capital city of Belgium. • Brussels serves as the political and administrative center of the EU where important institutions such as the European Commission, the Council of the European Union, and the European Council hold their meetings. • The city is considered the de facto capital of the EU due to the concentration of major EU institutions. • Brussels plays a vital role in shaping the policies and decisions of the European Union and acts as a hub for international diplomacy and cooperation.

questions.question: Which of the following former Indian Prime Minister did not receive the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. V.P. Singh . Among the given options, the former Indian Prime Minister who did not receive the Bharat Ratna is V. P. Singh. • Morarji Desai was conferred the Bharat Ratna in 1991 for his contributions to India’s freedom movement and governance. • Atal Bihari Vajpayee, one of the most respected leaders, was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2015 for his statesmanship and transformative leadership. • Lal Bahadur Shastri was honored posthumously with the Bharat Ratna in 1966 after his sudden demise in Tashkent; he is remembered for the slogan “Jai Jawan Jai Kisan.” • V. P. Singh, despite serving as the 7th Prime Minister (1989–1990) and implementing the Mandal Commission recommendations, did not receive this highest civilian honor.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian classical dance forms is interpreted as the dance of the celestial enchantress of Hindu mythology?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Mohiniyattam. • The Indian classical dance form interpreted as the "dance of the celestial enchantress" of Hindu mythology is Mohiniyattam. • The term Mohiniyattam is derived from the words Mohini, which means a woman who mesmerises the onlookers and Aattam, which means graceful and sensuous body movements. • Mohiniyattam is a famous classical dance of the southwestern state of Kerala, India. • This dance form is recognised by the Sangeet Natak Akademi as one of the 8 classical dance forms of India. • The famous exponents of Mohiniyattam are Bharati Shivaji, Sunanda Nair, and Gopika Varma Other Option: Bharatnatyam: • Origin: Tamil Nadu • Popular Exponents: Rukmini Devi Arundale, Yamini Krishnamurthy, Alarmel Valli. Kathak: • Origin: Uttar Pradesh • Popular Exponents: Birju Maharaj, Sitara Devi, Shovana Narayan. Sattriya: • Origin: Assam • Popular Exponents: Guru Jatin Goswami, Anita Sharma, Sharodi Saikia.

questions.question: In cricket, a full-pitched ball aimed at the batsman’s toes or on the crease line is called ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Yorker. In cricket, a full-pitched ball aimed at the batsman's toes or on the crease line is called a Yorker. It is one of the most effective deliveries, often used to dismiss batsmen, particularly in the final overs of a match.

questions.question: What is the theme of the World Day to Combat Desertification and Drought 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. "Restore the Land. Unlock the Opportunities." • Observed on June 17, the World Day to Combat Desertification and Drought raises awareness on land degradation and promotes sustainable land management. • The 2025 theme, “Restore the Land. Unlock the Opportunities,” highlights the economic and environmental benefits of land restoration. • With 1.5 billion hectares needing revival by 2030, the campaign urges global financial commitment. • India is aiming to restore 26 million hectares by 2030. • Initiatives like National Action Programme to Combat Desertification (NAPCD) and international cooperation underscore India’s proactive role in combating desertification. About World Day to Combat Desertification and Drought: • Each year, a country is selected to host global celebrations for Desertification and Drought Day. • The host country works with the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD). • This year's (2025) celebrations took place in Colombia.

questions.question: "Recently, the UN General Assembly designated the International Day Against Unilateral Coercive Measures. On which date will it be observed?"
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 4th December. On June 17, 2025, the UN General Assembly designated December 4 as the International Day Against Unilateral Coercive Measures through Resolution A/79/L.93. Backed by 116 countries, the resolution aims to raise awareness of the humanitarian and legal concerns posed by sanctions imposed outside UN frameworks. Opposed by 51 nations, including the US and EU, it reflects a growing global divide. The day emphasizes respect for sovereignty, highlights the impact on civilians, and supports multilateralism under the UN Charter’s Article 41.

questions.question: Where is the Bonn Climate Change Conference 2025 being held?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Germany. The Bonn Climate Change Conference 2025, held from June 16 in Germany, is a vital UNFCCC meeting that lays technical groundwork for the year-end COP summit. Focused on science, implementation, and climate governance, it gathers over 5,000 experts, officials, and activists. Key issues include operationalizing the Global Goal on Adaptation, climate finance, and loss and damage mechanisms. Led by the SBI and SBSTA, the conference ensures continuity, accountability, and science-based decision-making in advancing global climate action.

questions.question: Where did Maruti Suzuki inaugurate India’s largest in-plant automobile railway siding in June 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Manesar. Maruti Suzuki inaugurated India’s largest in-plant automobile railway siding at its Manesar facility on June 17, 2025, aligning with the PM Gati Shakti plan. Built with Rs. 452 crore, it aims to dispatch 4.5 lakh vehicles annually, reducing 1.75 lakh tonnes of CO₂e emissions and saving 60 million litres of fuel yearly. The project enhances green logistics by targeting 35% rail-based dispatches by FY2030–31. This is MSIL’s second such facility, after Gujarat, showcasing its commitment to sustainable transportation.

questions.question: What is the cost of the newly launched FASTag-based Annual Pass for private vehicles like cars, jeeps, and vans, effective August 15, 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. ₹3,000. The Government of India has launched a FASTag-based Annual Pass for Rs. 3,000, effective August 15, 2025, for private vehicles like cars, jeeps, and vans. Valid for 200 trips or one year, it aims to simplify toll payments, reduce congestion, and eliminate disputes at plazas under 60 km apart. Available via the Rajmarg Yatra App and NHAI websites, the pass promotes digital mobility and cost-effective travel on National Highways, aligning with India’s push toward a seamless, cashless tolling system.

questions.question: Which country is partnering with India in the 8th edition of Exercise Shakti 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. France. The eighth edition of the Indo-French military exercise ‘Shakti’ begins on June 18, 2025, at Camp Larzac, France. A 90-member Indian Army team, led by the Jammu and Kashmir Rifles, will train alongside French troops from the elite 13th DBLE. The exercise aims to boost interoperability, focusing on counter-terrorism, urban warfare, and UN Chapter VII peacekeeping operations. Conducted in semi-urban terrain, it includes joint planning, mission execution, and humanitarian tasks, fostering tactical coordination and mutual trust between the two nations.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Chairman of the revamped National Security Advisory Board (NSAB)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Alok Joshi. India has overhauled its National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) following the Pahalgam terror attack and rising tensions with Pakistan. Alok Joshi, former R&AW chief, has been appointed Chairman. The restructuring aims to strengthen national security and improve responses to terrorism. The revamped board includes senior officers from the defence, intelligence, and police sectors. It will advise the government on national security, military strategies, and diplomatic matters, focusing on countering terrorism and enhancing defence frameworks.

questions.question: Which scheme is the Greenhouse Gas Emissions Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025 linked to?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS). • India has proposed the Greenhouse Gas Emissions Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025, to regulate emissions intensity across key energy-intensive industries, supporting its climate goals under the Paris Agreement. • These rules, linked to the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), set reduction targets for industries like cement, aluminium, and paper. • Industries exceeding targets can earn carbon credits, which are tradable on the Indian Carbon Market. • The rules aim to reduce India’s GHG emissions intensity by 45% by 2030.

questions.question: Who announced the deployment of the 34 supercomputers under the National Supercomputing Mission recently?
options: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). • The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (Meity) announced the deployment of 34 supercomputers under the National Supercomputing Mission (NSM), with a combined computing power of 35 Petaflops. • These supercomputers, installed at academic and research institutions, serve over 10,000 researchers and start-ups. • NSM aims to strengthen India's supercomputing capabilities, with a focus on indigenisation. • The initiative has also led to the development of the Trinetra communication system and the Project AIRAWAT AI infrastructure.

questions.question: Where was the seventh edition of the Khelo India Youth Games inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Patna. The seventh edition of the Khelo India Youth Games was inaugurated in Patna, Bihar, on May 4, 2025. Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually opened the event, with Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar and other dignitaries attending the ceremony at the Patliputra Sports Complex. This marked Bihar's debut as host for the Games, which are scheduled from May 4 to 15, 2025, across multiple cities in the state, including Rajgir, Gaya, Bhagalpur, and Begusarai. Select events like shooting, gymnastics, and cycling are being held in New Delhi due to infrastructure considerations . The Games feature more than 10,000 participants, including over 6,000 athletes competing in 28 disciplines, including the debut of esports as a demonstration sport. The 6th edition of the Khelo India Youth Games was held from 19th January to 31st January 2024 across four cities in Tamil Nadu. The Khelo India Youth Games is a national multi-sport event for athletes under 18, launched in 2018 to promote grassroots sports. Organized by the Sports Ministry, it features disciplines like athletics, swimming, and wrestling.
